heading: Limitation of medical, surgical or hospital services
label: 35 Limitation of medical, surgical or hospital services
text: Hospitalization of the dependents of naval and Marine Corps personnel and of the persons outside the naval service mentioned in shall be furnished only for acute medical and surgical conditions, exclusive of nervous, mental, or contagious diseases or those requiring domiciliary care. Routine dental care, other than dental prosthesis and orthodontia, may be furnished to such persons who are outside the naval service under the same conditions as are prescribed in for hospital and dispensary care for such persons.section 34 of this titlesection 34 of this title
notes: Amendments1986— amended second sentence generally. Prior to amendment, second sentence read as follows: “Dental treatment shall be administered only as an adjunct to inpatient hospital care and shall not include dental prosthesis or orthodontia.”Pub. L. 99–251

Partial Repeal; Effective Date, , repealed this section except insofar as it relates to persons outside the Naval Service mentioned in . Section 307 of , , provided that such repeal was effective six months after .Act June 7, 1956, ch. 374, § 306(2)70 Stat. 254section 34 of this titleact June 7, 1956, ch. 37470 Stat. 254June 7, 1956
